source_file: The corporate social credit system in China and its transnational impact.txt
text:
The lack of trust and unethical practices for proﬁt maximisation
   are perceived as a problem within the Chinese society (1585–1586) and this trumps concerns for
   privacy—also because it is believed that the security apparatus has access to this type of data
   already (1586); Rachel Botsman, ‘Big Data meets Big Brother as China moves to rate its citizens’
   (Wired, 21 October 2017), online:www.wired.co.uk/article/chinese-government-social-credit-score-
   privacy-invasion. On the other hand, previous attempts to establish ‘morality ﬁles’ were mocked
   and the pitfalls of such endeavours publicly discussed. Min Jiang, ‘A Brief Prehistory of China’s
   Social Credit System’ (2020) 5 Communication & the Public 93, 96. 298         S. JAKOB
